description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline using the silentstorm/golang-coverage Docker image and setting GIT_STRATEGY to none and PROJECT_PATH to gitlab.com/silentteacup/congo. Define stages build and test, and a global before_script that runs go version, exports GO_PROJECT_DIR, clones the repository, checks out the commit, lists dependencies via ./_scripts/list_deps.sh, echoes them, and runs go get -d on the listed deps. Add a build-project job in the build stage that executes go build ./... . Add a test-project job in the test stage that runs goverage -coverprofile=coverage.out ./..., then go tool cover -html coverage.out -o coverage.html, go tool cover -func=coverage.out, and copies the coverage files to $CI_PROJECT_DIR, always publishing coverage.out and coverage.html as artifacts and extracting total coverage with the regex /total:\s+\(statements\)\s+\d+\.\d+%/. Add a lint-dupl job in the test stage that runs gometalinter --config='gometalinter_dupl.conf' $GO_PROJECT_DIR/... | sort -t: -k '1,1' -k '2,2g' -k '3,3g' > $CI_PROJECT_DIR/linter.log, allows failure, and on failure uploads linter.log as a two‑week artifact and prints errors and warnings via an after_script. Add a lint-source job in the test stage that runs gometalinter --config='gometalinter.conf' $GO_PROJECT_DIR/... | sort -t: -k '1,1' -k '2,2g' -k '3,3g' > $CI_PROJECT_DIR/linter.log with the same failure handling and after_script.
